import io
import string
import list

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
end

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut skipping = true
    while skipping
      if i >= n
        skipping = false
      else
        match string.byte_at(s, i)
        when Some(b)
          if is_ws(b)
            i = i + 1
          else
            skipping = false
          end
        when None
          skipping = false
        end
      end
    end
    if i < n
      let start = i
      mut scanning = true
      while scanning
        if i >= n
          scanning = false
        else
          match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(b)
            if is_ws(b)
              scanning = false
            else
              i = i + 1
            end
          when None
            scanning = false
          end
        end
      end
      let token = string.substring(s, start, i)
      match string.parse_int(token)
      when Some(v)
        result = list.push(result, v)
      when None
        ()
      end
    end
  end
  result
end

fn emit_pairs(_ a: List<Int>, _ b: List<Int>) -> Unit
  let n = list.len(a)
  mut i = 0
  mut stop = false
  while i < n and not stop
    match list.get(a, i)
    when Some(x)
      match list.get(b, i)
      when Some(y)
        let s = x + y
        println("#{x}+#{y}=#{s}")
      when None
        stop = true
      end
    when None
      stop = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line2)
    let xs = parse_ints(line1)
    let ys = parse_ints(line2)
    emit_pairs(xs, ys)
  when None
    ()
  end
when None
  ()
end
